tools.name: host_getClient
tools.description: Fetch one client with document progress, derived status and assigned staff.

tools.name: host_getDashboard
tools.description: Firm-wide dashboard metrics: how many clients are missing documents, documents outstanding vs received, and the nearest filing deadline with its client.

tools.name: host_listActivity
tools.description: Firm-wide activity feed, newest first: uploads received, documents verified or rejected, messages sent, deadlines approaching.

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return; omit for the full feed

tools.name: host_listClientDocuments
tools.description: List one client's requested tax documents (their checklist) with per-document status, uploaded file and any rejection note.

tools.name: host_listClientMessages
tools.description: Read one client's message thread with the firm, oldest first.

tools.name: host_listClients
tools.description: List the firm's clients with document progress, derived status and assigned staff. Filter to clients still missing documents or search by name.

filter.description: Set to missing_docs to return only clients with at least one outstanding document

q.description: Case-insensitive search on business name or contact name, e.g. 'rivera'

tools.name: host_listDeadlines
tools.description: All clients ordered by filing deadline (soonest first), each with document progress and the kinds of documents still missing. Use this to see who is at risk of missing their deadline.

tools.name: host_resetDemo
tools.description: Demo control: discard all changes and restore the seeded opening state (8 clients missing documents). Returns fresh dashboard metrics.

tools.name: host_sendClientMessage
tools.description: Send a message to a client as the firm. This is what the firm uses to chase outstanding documents; the client sees it in their portal.

author.description: Staff member sending the message; defaults to the signed-in user, Maya Alvarez

tools.name: host_setDocumentStatus
tools.description: Advance a document through its lifecycle: 'receive' marks a missing document as uploaded (attaching a file name), 'verify' confirms an uploaded document is correct, 'reject' returns a wrong upload to missing with a reason the client sees. Verify and reject only apply to documents in status received or needs_review.

docId.description: Document request id belonging to that client

fileName.description: Uploaded file name (receive only); a plausible default is generated if omitted

reason.description: Why the document was rejected (reject only); shown to the client until they re-upload

tools.name: host_simulateClientUpload
tools.description: Demo control: simulate a client uploading a file for a missing document. variant 'correct' lands the document in status received; variant 'wrong' (e.g. a personal bank statement instead of the business one) lands it in needs_review with an explanatory note. Also records a client-to-firm message and activity.

clientId.description: Client doing the uploading, e.g. cl_rivera

docId.description: Document request being fulfilled; must belong to the client and be in status missing

variant.description: correct: plausible right file -> received. wrong: mismatched file -> needs_review with a note

fileName.description: Uploaded file name; a plausible default is generated if omitted
